steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the overall structure of the text. Map it in two parts: (1) wheel-fashioned ceramics are "overemphasized," contributing to the idea that technological change always brings greater complexity; (2) Erb-Satullo's research introduces "an important exception" — gold metallurgy in the Caucasus actually became simpler over time. The structure is: flawed widespread interpretation, then a counterexample that challenges it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'summarizes findings of several studies... contradicts those findings': The passage mentions only one example (wheel ceramics) and one researcher (Erb-Satullo), not several studies whose findings are contradicted by newer research. ✗
  • Choice B — 'near-consensus... controversial nature of a study': The passage never says Erb-Satullo's study is controversial or disputed by others — it is simply presented as an exception. ✗
  • Choice C — 'particular interpretation has been perpetuated... counterexample demonstrating the interpretation isn't always accurate': The "overemphasized" idea (technology always adds complexity) is the perpetuated interpretation; Erb-Satullo's gold metallurgy data is the counterexample showing that interpretation fails in at least one case. ✓
  • Choice D — 'advances a claim... critiques a contrasting claim': The passage does not pit one researcher's claim against another's in a critique. Erb-Satullo is not criticized — his work is used as evidence. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
